title: cephalaria leucantha var. fragosoana
intro: Cephalaria leucantha var. fragosoana, also known as White Horehound, is a flowering plant species in the family Caprifoliaceae, native to the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally, White Horehound has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been valued for its health benefits, which include relief from congestion, sore throats, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. In herbal preparations, White Horehound is often used as a tea or infusion, and can also be found in combination with other herbs in cough syrups and remedies for respiratory issues.
